skill.name: make-interfaces-feel-better
skill.description: Design engineering principles for making interfaces feel polished. Covers concentric border radius, optical alignment, shadows over borders, interruptible animations, staggered enter/exit transitions, contextual icon animations, font smoothing, tabular numbers, text wrapping, image outlines, scale on press, minimum hit areas, and GPU-composited performance patterns. Provides review checklists and before/after output format.
skill.summary: Collection of UI design engineering principles for polished interfaces, covering animations, typography, surfaces, and performance.
skill.author: jakubkrehel

security_audit.summary: All 270 static analysis findings are false positives. The 248 'shell backtick execution' detections are markdown code fences (```css, ```tsx) and inline code backticks used for documentation formatting, not actual shell commands. The 22 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are CSS filter: blur(4px) references for visual effects, not cryptographic operations. The system/network reconnaissance detections are CSS property references and markdown links. This skill is a documentation-only reference with no executable code, network calls, or file system access.

content.user_title: Polish your UI with design engineering principles
content.value_statement: Many developers struggle to make their interfaces feel polished and professional. This skill provides specific, actionable design principles for animations, typography, surfaces, and performance that you can apply immediately to any frontend project.
actual_capabilities: Review UI code and provide specific improvements for concentric border radius calculations
Identify and fix optical alignment issues in icons, buttons, and play button triangles
Replace hard borders with layered box-shadow patterns for adaptive depth in light and dark mode
Implement interruptible CSS transitions instead of keyframe animations for interactive states
Apply split and staggered enter animations with correct timing for content sections
Add contextual icon animations with exact scale, opacity, and blur values
limitations: Only provides CSS and React/Motion code examples, not other framework implementations
Does not generate new UI designs, only reviews and improves existing interfaces
Cannot test or verify the actual runtime performance of suggested changes
Design principles assume standard web UI patterns and may not suit highly specialized interfaces

use_cases.title: UI code review for frontend developers
use_cases.description: A frontend developer runs a review of their pull request UI code and receives specific before/after suggestions for border radius, shadows, typography, and animation improvements.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building React or Next.js applications

use_cases.title: Design system audit for design engineers
use_cases.description: A design engineer evaluates their component library against the skill principles to identify inconsistent border radii, missing animations, and performance issues across the system.
use_cases.target_user: Design engineers maintaining component libraries or design systems

use_cases.title: UI polish pass for product teams
use_cases.description: A product team preparing for launch runs a comprehensive polish pass on their interface, catching issues like missing font smoothing, layout shift from proportional numbers, and non-interruptible animations.
use_cases.target_user: Product teams doing pre-launch quality assurance on user interfaces

prompt_templates.title: Review a single UI component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the following button component using the make-interfaces-feel-better principles. Check border radius, optical alignment, shadows, scale on press, and hit area. Show changes as a before/after table.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick component review for a specific UI element

prompt_templates.title: Full UI page polish review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the following page component using all applicable principles from make-interfaces-feel-better: concentric border radius, optical alignment, shadows over borders, font smoothing, tabular numbers, text wrapping, image outlines, and minimum hit area. Group findings by principle.

prompt_templates.title: Animation audit for interactive elements
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the animations in this component using the make-interfaces-feel-better principles. Check for interruptible transitions vs keyframes, split and staggered enter animations, subtle exit animations, contextual icon animations with correct scale/opacity/blur values, and scale on press. Verify no transition:all usage.

prompt_templates.title: Design system consistency check
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this design system's component set against the make-interfaces-feel-better principles. Identify inconsistent border radius patterns, missing optical alignment adjustments, non-standard animation values, and any transition:all or will-change:all violations. Prioritize fixes by impact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Cross-component consistency audit for design systems

output_examples.input: A card component with rounded-xl on both the card and its inner button, using a solid border for the container.
output: The card and inner button share the same rounded-xl radius which makes the interface feel off. Apply concentric border radius: set the card to rounded-2xl (20px) and the inner button to rounded-lg (8px) with 8px padding between them.

Replace the solid border on the card with layered box-shadow for depth: use 0px 0px 0px 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) for the ring effect, combined with 0px 1px 2px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.06) for lift. This adapts to any background automatically.

output_examples.input: An icon button that toggles between a play and pause icon with no transition animation.
output: Add contextual icon animation using opacity, scale, and blur. The entering icon should animate from opacity 0, scale 0.25, blur 4px to opacity 1, scale 1, blur 0px. The exiting icon reverses these values.

Use a spring transition with duration 0.3 and bounce 0. If the project does not use Motion, keep both icons in the DOM with one absolutely positioned and cross-fade using CSS transitions with cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1).

best_practices: Apply concentric border radius to all nested rounded elements: outer radius equals inner radius plus padding between them
Use CSS transitions for interactive state changes instead of keyframe animations so elements can be interrupted mid-animation
Add image outlines using pure black or pure white at 0.1 opacity to maintain consistent depth across light and dark modes
anti_patterns: Using the same border radius on nested elements without accounting for padding between them
Applying transition: all or will-change: all which forces the browser to watch every property and wastes GPU memory
Animating a single large container on page load instead of splitting content into staggered semantic chunks

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill provides code examples for CSS, Tailwind CSS, and React with Framer Motion. The principles apply to any web UI framework.

faq.question: Can this skill generate new UI designs?
faq.answer: No. The skill reviews and improves existing UI code. It is designed for polish passes and code review, not visual design from scratch.

faq.question: What is concentric border radius?
faq.answer: It is the rule that outer border radius must equal inner border radius plus padding. For example, a card with 8px padding and an inner element with 12px radius needs a 20px outer radius.

faq.question: When should I use box-shadow instead of borders?
faq.answer: Use box-shadow for element depth on cards, buttons, and containers. Keep borders for layout separators like dividers and table cell boundaries.

faq.question: How do I prevent animations from playing on page load?
faq.answer: Set initial={false} on AnimatePresence in Framer Motion. This prevents enter animations from firing on first render for elements that are already in their default state.

faq.question: What is the minimum hit area for interactive elements?
faq.answer: Interactive elements need at least 40 by 40 pixels. If the visible element is smaller, extend the hit area with a pseudo-element. Never let hit areas of two elements overlap.
